name: Angel Food Candy
recipeInstructions:
NOTE: The first 4-ounces of semi-sweet chocolate is in block style.
Also, the chocolate chips should be semi-sweet.  The paraffin
wax should be a 2 1/2 x 3-inch piece that has been cut up.
1. Combine the corn syrup and brown sugar in a 4-quart heavy Dutch oven.
2. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture boils.
3. Continue cooking the mixture until it reaches the hard crack stage (300°F) on the candy thermometer, stirring occasionally.
4. Remove from the heat and carefully stir in the baking soda, being careful as the mixture will foam up.
5. Pour the mixture at once into a greased 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan.
6. When cool, turn out of the pan and break into pieces using a meat mallet or wooden spoon.
7. Combine the semi-sweet chocolate pieces, chocolate chips, butter, and paraffin wax in the top of a double boiler.
8. Place over simmering water, stirring until melted. Remove from the heat but keep over the hot water.
9. Dip the candy into the chocolate using a large cooking fork.
10. Place on waxed paper-lined baking sheets. Let stand until chocolate is set.
11. Store the candy in a cool place in covered containers. If desired, do not coat the candy with chocolate.
Candy can be stored for several months in the freezer.
Makes 3 pounds of candy.
